title: encelia canescens var. tomentosa
intro: Encelia canescens var. tomentosa, also known as the bush sunflower or velvety Brittlebrush, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Encelia canescens var. tomentosa are said to include relief from pain, reduced inflammation, and improved wound healing, making it a valuable herb for topical applications. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, salves, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
